title: Count max Subarray

content.problemStatement:
Given an array arr[]arr[]arr[] of NNN integers and an integer KKK. The task is to find the number of subarray with a maximum value equal to KKK.


Input Format

The first line contains two integers NNN , KKK - the size of `arr` and the maximum value that should be in the
subarray.
The second line contains nnn integers arr[1],arr[2],…,arr[n]arr[1],arr[2],…,arr[n]arr[1],arr[2],…,arr[n] — the elements of arrarrarr.


Output Format
Print the number of subarrays having KKK as the maximum value.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains two integers N and K – the size of the array and the required maximum value. The second line contains N space‑separated integers denoting the array elements.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the number of subarrays whose maximum element is exactly K.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The subarrays whose maximum equals 3 are [2,1,3], [1,3] and [3].
Only the two single‑element subarrays containing 1 have maximum 1.
The subarrays whose maximum equals 5 are [1,2,3,4,5], [2,3,4,5], [3,4,5], [4,5] and [5].

solution.approach: A subarray has maximum exactly K iff all its elements are <= K and it contains at least one element equal to K. Count the number of subarrays where every element is <= K (call it F(K)) and subtract the number of subarrays where every element is <= K‑1 (call it F(K‑1)). The difference F(K)‑F(K‑1) gives the required answer. To compute F(limit) we scan the array, split it into maximal contiguous blocks of elements <= limit and for each block of length L add L*(L+1)/2 subarrays. This runs in O(N) per test case and uses O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) for the whole input.

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{private static long countLE(int[] a,int limit){long ans=0;int n=a.length;int i=0;while(i<n){if(a[i]>limit){i++;continue;}long cnt=0;while(i<n&&a[i]<=limit){cnt++;i++;}ans+=cnt*(cnt+1)/2;}return ans;}public static void main(String[] args)throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());while(T-->0){StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());int N=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());int K=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());int[] arr=new int[N];st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());for(int i=0;i<N;i++)arr[i]=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());long leK=countLE(arr,K);long leKm1=countLE(arr,K-1);sb.append(leK-leKm1).append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
